title: Insert Interval
description: Given a list of intervals where each interval is represented as a list of two integers, and a new interval, you need to insert the new interval into the list of intervals and merge any overlapping intervals. Return the resulting list of intervals sorted by their start time.

examples.explanation: The new interval [2, 5] overlaps with [1, 3]. Therefore, we merge them to form [1, 5]. The interval [6, 9] remains unchanged.
The new interval [4, 8] overlaps with [3, 5] and [6, 7] and merges them into [3, 10]. The other intervals remain unchanged.
The new interval [2, 3] is completely contained within [1, 5], so there is no change in the result.

constraints: The input intervals list must be non-empty.
Each interval has two integers representing its start and end, where start <= end.
hints: Consider how to identify overlaps between intervals.
You may need to iterate through the list of intervals to find the correct position for insertion.
Remember to merge intervals that overlap after inserting the new interval.
Think about edge cases such as inserting an interval at the beginning or end of the list.
